trade.summary: Italia and Giappone have a substantial trade relationship. Italia exports luxury goods, fashion, food, and machinery; Giappone exports vehicles, electronics, and machinery. Italia's luxury brands have large Giapponeese market share. The relationship is complementary with both countries exporting high-value manufactured goods.

diplomatic.summary: Italia and Giappone are G7 partners with strong diplomatic ties. Both are liberal democracies and members of key multilateral institutions. The G7 provides a strong framework for alignment. Italia-Giappone relations were elevated by both countries' participation in the GCAP fighter jet program (with the Regno Unito). High-level diplomatic contacts are frequent.

military.summary: Growing defense cooperation anchored by the Global Combat Air Programme (GCAP), a major trilateral program with Italia, Giappone, and the Regno Unito to develop a next-generation fighter aircraft by 2035. This is a landmark defense partnership. Both countries are Stati Uniti allies and increasingly coordinate on Indo-Pacific security.

financial.summary: Significant bilateral investment. Giapponeese automakers (Toyota, Honda, others) have Italian market presence; Italian luxury and design firms have Giapponeese partners. Financial ties are robust through G7 coordination mechanisms.

energy.summary: Both are major energy importers. Some cooperation on energy transition and hydrogen technology. No direct bilateral energy trade of significance.

scientific.summary: Active scientific cooperation in physics, materials science, and space. Italia and Giappone collaborate through CERN and other multilateral scientific bodies. Joint research programs in advanced manufacturing and life sciences.
